_patch_meta.description: Session B closing patch for registry 211 — being. Records analysis document filename. Writes evidential status notes to all 54 active terms. Sets session_b_status to Analysis Complete.

updates.status_note: Core contra-wisdom term. 21 verses. Ignorance as a correctable inner state; ranges from culpable (Rom 10:3) to involuntary (1 Tim 1:13) to pastoral prevention.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Col 3:21). Discouragement as inner-being state produced by parental provocation. Thin data; connection plausible.
Perplexity as inner-being state. 5 verses. 2 Cor 4:8 anchor: perplexed but not in despair.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Rom 10:20). Bold speech; inner-being dimension present but thin.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (2 Tim 1:7) but theologically dense: deilia explicitly named as a spirit the Spirit displaces. Confirmed despite thin data.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Joh 14:27). Inner fear addressed by Jesus. PH2-211-004. Thin data.
Cowardice as inner-being disposition. 3 verses including Rev 21:8 — most severe canonical statement. Jesus rebukes it as little faith (Mat 8:26, Mar 4:40).
Intensive perplexity. 5 verses. Consistently a response to divine action exceeding comprehension.
Fervency in spirit. 2 verses. Inner spiritual intensity described as burning (Act 18:25; Rom 12:11).
Eager desire / zealous intensity. 11 verses. Double semantic range: earnest desire (positive) and jealousy (negative) — same inner force.
Settled confidence. 6 verses. Pauline: confidence as disposition not dependent on circumstances (2 Cor 5:6-8).
Imparted courage. 7 verses. Six of seven are Jesus speaking directly: Take heart. Courage as imparted inner steadiness.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Act 7:19). Shrewd/cunning disposition; here belonging to an oppressor. Inner-being connection plausible but thin.
Becoming foolish. 4 verses. Rom 1:22 pivotal: claiming wisdom they became fools. Process of inner-being inversion.
Foolish as character type. 12 verses. God chose the moros to shame the wise (1 Cor 1:27).
Boldness / confident openness. 31 verses. Acts (evangelical) and 1 John (before God on the day of judgment, grounded in perfected love). Most substantial boldness term.
Bold proclamation. 9 verses. Parresia in action (Acts). Inner-being disposition expressed through speech.
Wisdom. 49 verses. Two-wisdom contrast (James 3), Christ as wisdom (1 Cor 1:30), wisdom as Spirit-given capacity. Most substantial Greek wisdom term.
extracted_thin. 2 verses. Scriptures making wise for salvation (2 Tim 3:15). PH2-211-003. Thin data.
Wise. 19 verses (PH2-211-002). God alone truly wise (Rom 16:27). Theological inversion of human wisdom.
Diligent / make every effort. 11 verses. Committed inner disposition expressed in paraenetic contexts.
Earnest. 2 verses. Character description of Titus (2 Cor 8:17, 22).
Diligence / earnestness. 12 verses. Inner quality of earnest commitment (2 Cor 7:11; Heb 6:11; 2 Pet 1:5).
Daring / be bold. 15 verses. The inner overcoming of restraint to act. Range from daring to ask to daring to die (Rom 5:7).
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Rom 15:15). Comparative form; inner-being dimension present but thin.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (2 Pet 2:10). Bold and willful as negative character type. Thin data.
Puffed up / conceited. 3 verses. Pride as inflation; 1 Tim 3:6, 6:4, 2 Tim 3:4. Conceit as end-times character marker.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Rom 12:3). Thinking too highly of oneself; sober judgment as the counter-disposition. Thin data.
Haughty / proud (inner-being subset). 11 verses of 198 occ (PH2-211-001). Lk 16:15 anchor: what is exalted among men is abomination to God.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (1 Tim 6:17). Arrogance disposition. Thin data.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (1 Cor 14:20). Mind/thinking as inner capacity. PH2-211-005. Thin data but clarifies phroneo root.
Set the mind / orientation of thinking. 19 verses. Mind set on flesh vs. Spirit (Rom 8:5); having the mind of Christ (Phil 2:5). Key inner-operations term.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Tit 3:8). Inner attentiveness to good works. Thin data.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (1 Cor 4:4). Self-awareness as inner capacity; self-assessment does not constitute acquittal. Thin data.
Stupid / brutish. 5 verses. Inner incapacity to know; Psa 73:22 notable as self-assessment of prior brutish ignorance before God.
Be anxious. 7 verses. Worried preoccupation distinguished from stark fear. Relational objects: person, outcome, enemy.
Anxiety. 6 verses. Pro 12:25 anchor: anxiety weighs the heart down; good word lifts it. Body-inner interaction.
Wise / chakam. 127 verses. Core Hebrew wisdom term. Enacted understanding: royal counsellor, craftsman, sage, moral exemplar. Wisdom is a formed inner capacity.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Psa 119:70). Heart unfeeling like fat — insensitivity as inner state using bodily metaphor. Thin data.
Be willing / content / pleased. 19 verses. Volitional inner disposition of acquiescence. Covers human willingness and divine pleasure.
Be disheartened. 3 verses. Eze 13:22 significant: false prophets disheartened the righteous — inner state produced by external agents.
Disheartened (adj). 3 verses. Same verses as H3512A.
Be humble / subdued. 32 verses. Predominantly Ring 2. Humility as crisis-response posture when confronted by divine reality. 2 Ch 7:14 anchor.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Jer 10:8). Stupidity in context of idol-worship. Thin data.
Prudent / crafty — arum. 11 verses. Double semantic range: prudence (Proverbs positive) and craftiness (Gen 3:1 negative). Morally neutral capacity.
Be shrewd — aram. 5 verses. Inner capacity for strategic perception.
Craftiness / prudence — ormah. 5 verses. Noun form of aram. Positively: wisdom imparts ormah (Pro 1:4, 8:5, 8:12).
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Gen 49:4). Recklessness as contra-character disposition. Thin data.
Walk humbly — tsana'. 1 verse (Mic 6:8). Rare; pivotal ethical summary. Retained.
Humble — tsanua'. 1 verse (Pro 11:2). Humility paired with wisdom.
extracted_thin. 1 verse (Dan 5:9). Perplexity at divine writing. Thin data.
Be mad. 6 verses. Range: genuine madness (divine judgment), feigned madness (1 Sam 21), prophetic behaviour read as madness (Hos 9:7).
Madness. 3 verses. Deu 28:28: paired with blindness and confusion of mind as divine judgments.

extracted_thin. 2 verses (Song 4:2; 6:6). Both verses describe physical twinning of sheep. No inner-being connection found in corpus. Retained on retention default. Researcher review recommended for delete.

operations.description: Session B analysis complete for registry 211 — being. Narrative document: wa-211-being-analysis-20260327.docx.
